Currently FLUX.1-dev has two correctness issues that deteriorate the output quality. This can be seen as a blocky output image and the inability to generate higher resolution (2048 * 2048) images that it should be able to. This PR adds fixes to the two issues, improving the output quality

vllm.title: [EPLB] Remove main waits in case of slow EPLB
vllm.author: ilmarkov
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
We remove synchronization between main and eplb async thread with cuda event.
Currently main thread is only notified on buffer readiness when the weight transfers are scheduled on the eplb stream. So main stream waits on that. If these transfers are too slow it will impact the performance of

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Suppress spurious CPU KV cache warning in `launch render`
vllm.author: sagearc

vllm launch render does no inference and never allocates KV cache, but on CPU machines it was printing a misleading "VLLM_CPU_KVCACHE_SPACE not set. Using 32 GiB for KV cache" warning on startup. This PR suppresses it.
Tested by running vllm launch render --model Qwen/Qwen2-VL-7B-Instruct-AWQ on a C

vllm.title: [Core] add option to schedule requests based on full ISL
vllm.author: DanBlanaru
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The default behaviour of vLLM is to schedule requests if the first chunk of a request fits.
Observed problematic behavior (ISL numbers are mocked because this is customer data): When 4×100k ISL requests already occupy ~90% of the KV cache, the scheduler still admits a 5th 100k ISL request. I

vllm.title: [Revert] Remove CUDA torch fallbacks for fp8_mqa_logits/fp8_paged_mqa_logits_torch function
vllm.author: chaunceyjiang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Revet #35271
The original PR #35271 was intended to allow dsv3.2 to run even when deep_gemm is not installed or on lower-end GPUs such as A800.
